title: Commerce to pre-test frontier AI as four jurisdictions act - TCR 05/06/26
description:
The Commerce Department announced pre-deployment testing for frontier AI models from Google, Microsoft, and xAI, NERC issued a rare Level 3 alert mandating seven grid actions by August 3 over data center load oscillations, and the Financial Stability Board warned that AI now accounts for more than a third of private credit deals globally, up from 17% over the previous five years.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-6-2026/

0:00 Four jurisdictions, one week of litigation
0:55 Commerce reverses the AI Action Plan
7:12 FSB names AI infrastructure debt
10:05 Four Jurisdictions, One Week

This episode covers the Commerce reversal of the AI Action Plan posture, NERC's Level 3 alert and the bulk power planning architecture being rewritten, the FSB systemic-risk warning on AI infrastructure debt, the Meta Llama copyright class action from five major publishers including Macmillan and Elsevier, the Apple $250 million Siri settlement, the Pennsylvania Character.AI medical impersonation lawsuit covering 45,500 interactions, and Greg Brockman's sworn testimony about the 2017 Hillsborough mansion meeting that produced OpenAI's for-profit structure.

The Commerce announcement is the most direct reversal yet of the federal AI policy posture established less than a year ago. The companies named are the same ones that accepted the Pentagon's "all lawful purposes" terms in February - the same week Anthropic refused and was designated a supply-chain risk. Pre-deployment review is becoming the federal default, with benchmark documents and evaluator roles that persist regardless of administration. NERC's Level 3 alert mandates that grid entities collect minimum and maximum consumption data, IT-versus-cooling ratios, and load behavior characteristics from data centers - forcing into the public planning record information that previously lived inside private utility contracts. The FSB warning makes AI infrastructure debt visible to the same regulators who built the post-2008 architecture. And in four jurisdictions on one week, the verification gap is closing through litigation - copyright, consumer protection, professional licensing, and energy procurement law all reaching for accountability the deployment cycle outpaced.
